state_history.note: Extracted from broker email (Jim Patterson, Carolina Brokers LLC). 24-unit self-service laundromat, downtown Charlotte, fully unattended, revenue $385K TTM, SDE $105K, asking $385K

Deal-Evaluator: 3.67x SDE multiple (within FAIR range 2.5-4.0x for laundromats). Seller archetype: Genuine Retiree (age 68, offering seller financing). Fully unattended = passive-eligible for H1B. Verdict: PURSUE WITH CONDITIONS. Opening offer position: 3.0x SDE (~$315K)

Market-Intel overlay complete: State risk LOW (NC business-friendly), industry trend STABLE, competitive density MODERATE (12 laundromats in Charlotte = 1 per 3,500 people). Verdict: GO. Card system 2021 (modern), downtown gentrification tailwind, lease expires 2028 (manageable)

Broker-Vetter: Jim Patterson (Carolina Brokers LLC) verified TIER 2, score 16/20. IBBA member, 12 years active, clean web presence, 28 reviews (4.3 avg), 2 laundromat deals in case studies, no disqualifiers. User elected to pursue outreach.

Broker-Comm: Initial inquiry drafted and user-approved for sending. Subject: 'Inquiry regarding Wash & Win Laundromat (Charlotte, NC)'. Request for CIM + NDA. Professional 185-word intro. Email sent to jim@carolinabrokers.com

operationals.equipment: 24-unit self-service + 4 commercial dryers, card system installed 2021
operationals.lease_status: landlord aware and supportive of sale

seller_profile.flexibility: Offering seller financing up to 20% of purchase price

communications.subject: Inquiry regarding Wash & Win Laundromat (Charlotte, NC)
communications.summary: Initial inquiry, professional intro, CIM + NDA request
communications.body_preview: Dear Jim, [Introduction as serious buyer]. [Request for Confidential Information Memo and NDA]. [Indication of timeline and seriousness]. [Professional sign-off]

market_intel.state_regulatory_notes: Business-friendly environment, reasonable workers compensation rates (1.2% of payroll), no aggressive regulatory environment
market_intel.industry_trend_notes: Fragmented market with no consolidation pressure, card/app system upgrades driving modest modernization

primary_coverage_risks: water damage (lint accumulation risk, manageable with maintenance)
slip and fall (typical for service businesses)
insurance_outlook.risk_mitigation: Fully unattended operation reduces premise liability exposure
recommended_coverage: Property Insurance (equipment + building if owned, lease if rented)

market_intel.competitive_density_analysis: Charlotte metro area has approximately 12 laundromats, equating to 1 per 3,500 households. Healthy density (fragmented market), not saturated.
tailwinds: Card/app system installed 2021 (modern payment verification, high revenue accuracy)
Downtown location (gentrification corridor, strong demographic trends)
Landlord aware and supportive (lease assignability confirmed pre-contact)
Fully unattended model (low operational risk, high passive fit)
4-year lease remaining (reasonable runway, landlord extension likely)
headwinds: Lease expires 2028 (8-year term is manageable, but finite runway requires planning)
market_intel.verdict_rationale: Low-risk state environment, stable industry fundamentals, healthy competitive position, exceptional absentee fit for H1B passive buyer

standard_10_percent.name: SBA 7(a) 90% LTV (Standard)
standard_10_percent.rationale: DSCR well above 1.25x minimum, exceptional cash-on-cash return, no manager salary needed

seller_financing_partial.name: Seller Financing Partial (Optional)
seller_financing_partial.rationale: SBA scenario already strong, seller financing only valuable if lender denies 90% LTV, which is unlikely for laundromat

financing.recommended_lender_rationale: Laundromat specialist, 60-90 day SBA approval timeline, familiarity with unattended operations

h1b_eligibility_assessment.compliance_notes: No active management required. Buyer can be fully passive, leveraging property management or automated systems.

required_conditions_before_offer: Confirm card system operational and reliability (payment processor track record)
Verify lease assignability with landlord in writing (critical for SBA)
financing.sba_approval_rationale: Established business, strong DSCR (1.87x), manageable owner-operator to absentee conversion, good collateral (equipment + lease)
next_steps_financing: Broker responds with CIM + operational data
Request: 3 years tax returns, lease copy, equipment list with ages
Contact Live Oak Bank for pre-qualification meeting
Prepare SBA loan package (1920 form, personal financial statement, business plan)

deal_assessment_summary.multiple_assessment: 3.67x SDE multiple is in upper-range FAIR territory for laundromats (2.5-4.0x typical). Slight premium justified by: modern card system, fully unattended, strong market, genuine retiree seller with financing flexibility
deal_assessment_summary.negotiation_strategy: Opening offer: 3.0x SDE ($315,000). Walk-away: 3.5x SDE ($367,500). Middle ground: 3.25x SDE ($341,250) if seller balks.
key_strengths: Fully unattended (perfect for H1B passive investor)
Modern card system (2021 install, revenue verification high)
Strong SDE relative to asking price (27% SDE margin)

key_risks_and_mitigants.mitigant: Landlord aware and supportive, 4 years is runway for ROI, landlord extension likely post-close

key_risks_and_mitigants.risk: Downtown location subject to gentrification volatility
key_risks_and_mitigants.mitigant: Gentrification is tailwind (customer base strength), laundromat demand stable

key_risks_and_mitigants.risk: Equipment age (card system 2021, no data on dryers/washers age)
key_risks_and_mitigants.mitigant: Request full equipment inventory in CIM, budget CapEx reserve ($5-10K annually)

deal_assessment_summary.recommended_next_action: Await broker reply with CIM (typically 3-5 business days). Once received, sign NDA, send targeted operational questions, request seller intro call.
